Building the future of
compliant machines.

Sunflower Robotics engineers compliant mechanisms — adaptive structures that sense, flex, and protect. Our beachhead is diabetic foot care. Our vision is every machine that touches a body.

01 — The Beachhead

Diabetic foot care.
The hardest problem first.

Half a billion people live with diabetes. 40% will develop a foot ulcer. Most of those ulcers were caused by pressure — pressure that the right insole could have redirected.

We chose this problem because it is urgent, underserved, and technically demanding. If we can solve compliant protection at this level of sensitivity, we can solve it anywhere.

See the clinical data →

02 — The Technology

Not foam.
Living geometry.

Our insoles use compliant mechanisms — flexure units engineered to deform predictably under load, redirecting pressure laterally away from at-risk zones.

This is the same class of mechanism that makes precision surgical tools, aerospace actuators, and next-generation robot joints possible. We've miniaturized it into an insole.

Read the patent →

03 — AI Reconstruction

Three photos.
Your exact geometry.

Our computer vision pipeline takes a plantar, dorsal, and medial photo — and outputs a watertight, personalized 3D foot model. No clinic. No scanner. No appointment.

Clinical measurements extracted automatically: Chippaux-Smirak Index, arch deformation profile, forefoot spread, toe deformities. The insole is a physical expression of your data.

Explore the pipeline →

One platform.
Many applications.

Compliant mechanism technology is not limited to insoles. The same IP that protects diabetic feet can protect robot hands, amputee residual limbs, and industrial workers. Diabetic care is our beachhead, not our ceiling.

01

Diabetic Orthotics

AI-personalized insoles that redistribute plantar pressure in real time, preventing the ulcers that lead to amputations.

Active · Beachhead
02

Prosthetics & Bracing

Compliant interfaces between prosthetic sockets and residual limbs — eliminating pressure sores and improving proprioception.

Roadmap · 2026
03

Robot End-Effectors

Soft grippers and compliant joints that let robots handle delicate objects — produce, glassware, human tissue — without rigid force control.

Roadmap · 2027

Traditional insoles vs. Sunflower

What matters Traditional orthotics Sunflower Robotics
Fit to your foot Generic sizing, average molds ✓AI-reconstructed from 3 photos
Pressure response Static — bounces force back up ✓Real-time lateral redistribution
Deformity support Not addressed by design ✓Geometry encoded for your profile
Clinical setup Expensive scanner or plaster cast ✓3 smartphone photos, no clinic visit
HSA / FSA eligible Rarely, varies by product ✓Yes — prescription pathway

Grounded in
clinical evidence.

85%

Of diabetic amputations are preceded by a foot ulcer — the majority developing from pressure hotspots that adaptive insoles can directly offload.

International Diabetes Federation
60–80%

Reduction in peak plantar pressure shown in literature for well-fitted custom orthotics. Our technology targets this threshold through real-time mechanism adaptation.

Journal of Foot and Ankle Research
3 photos

Is all our AI requires to reconstruct a clinically accurate 3D foot model — validated against Volumental ground-truth scans with sub-millimeter accuracy goals.

Sunflower Robotics · Internal Validation
